params.negative_prompt: no text, no words, no letters, no numbers, no digits, no labels, no stickers, no decals, no placards, no serial tags, no serial plates, no engraved plates, no warning signs, no warning plates, no stamped markings, no screen digits, no icon plates, no symbol plates, no interface readouts, no readable ui, no graph dashboard, no line chart hero, no cracked screen, no shattered glass, no exterior launch pad, no shuttle exterior hero, no fireball spectacle, no crowd scene, no hearing-room melodrama
params.note_text: Final v3 beat 04b deliverable. Preserve the mission command room and the smoke-pattern screen event without readable UI or spectacle.
params.accent_palette: pale gray, desaturated green, charcoal, smoke gray, warning amber
params.safe_zone_intent: keep the mission screen and seated command silhouettes above the subtitle band while leaving the top UI band calm
params.signal_anchor: mission command room registering the returning smoke pattern as a screen-borne failure signal
params.historical_anchor: as flame became visible again, Challenger's failure could be read as a monitored event inside mission command
params.surreal_breach: a diffuse smoke pattern echoes across one mission screen while the room itself remains procedural and calm

prompt_fragments.anchor_fragment: mission command room with one large overhead screen carrying a diffuse smoke-plume echo while seated operators remain subordinate below it
support_fragments: institutional consoles and restrained silhouettes held under the screen rather than competing with it
the smoke pattern reads as technical observation rather than cracked glass, graph UI, or exterior spectacle
directives: keep the room specific to mission command rather than a generic office meeting space
make the smoke pattern screen-borne and diffuse without plot lines or interface cues
hold the image inside procedural calm instead of catastrophe spectacle
